title: trianthema portulacastrum
intro: Trianthema portulacastrum, also known as purslane or pigweed, is an annual succulent plant belonging to the family Portulacaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of beta-carotene, vitamin E, and other nutrients. The health benefits of Trianthema portulacastrum include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as supporting immune function and reducing oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be consumed fresh or dried, and are often used in teas, infusions, or as a topping for salads.
